beginner.question: What are CSS @keyframes used for?
beginner.answer: @keyframes defines a sequence of animation steps by specifying property values at various points during the animation.

beginner.question: What is the animation-name property?
beginner.answer: animation-name specifies which @keyframes animation to apply to an element, matching the keyframe block's identifier.

beginner.question: What are keyframe selectors?
beginner.answer: Keyframe selectors are percentage values (0% to 100%) that define at which point in the animation each style change occurs.

beginner.question: What do 0% and 100% represent in keyframes?
beginner.answer: 0% is the start of the animation and 100% is the end; they can also be written using the keywords 'from' and 'to'.

beginner.question: How do you define a simple fade-in animation using @keyframes?
beginner.answer: @keyframes fadeIn { from { opacity: 0; } to { opacity: 1; } } and apply it with animation-name: fadeIn.

beginner.question: Can you have multiple keyframe selectors in one @keyframes rule?
beginner.answer: Yes, you can use any percentage values like 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100% to create multi-step animations.

beginner.question: What happens if you omit the 0% or 100% keyframe?
beginner.answer: If omitted, the browser uses the element's computed styles as the start (0%) or end (100%) values.

beginner.question: Can multiple properties be animated in one @keyframes rule?
beginner.answer: Yes, you can animate multiple CSS properties within a single @keyframes block by declaring them at each keyframe selector.

beginner.question: What is the syntax for defining two keyframe selectors with the same styles?
beginner.answer: You can group selectors: 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0; } to apply the same styles at multiple points.

beginner.question: Can @keyframes be defined inside media queries?
beginner.answer: No, @keyframes rules cannot be nested inside other at-rules like @media; they must be defined at the top level.

intermediate.question: How do you create a bounce animation using @keyframes?
intermediate.answer: Define keyframes at 0%, 50%, and 100% with translateY moving up and down, using ease-in/out timing for realistic bounce.

intermediate.question: Can you use CSS custom properties inside @keyframes?
intermediate.answer: Standard CSS custom properties work inside @keyframes; registered custom properties (via @property) can animate smoothly.

intermediate.question: What happens if two @keyframes have the same name?
intermediate.answer: The last defined @keyframes with that name in the cascade order takes precedence, overriding earlier definitions.

intermediate.question: How do keyframes interact with the will-change property?
intermediate.answer: will-change hints the browser about which properties will animate, allowing optimization before the keyframe animation starts.

intermediate.question: Can you use transform functions inside @keyframes?
intermediate.answer: Yes, transform is commonly animated in @keyframes, including multiple transform functions at different keyframe points.

intermediate.question: How do you create a color cycling animation with @keyframes?
intermediate.answer: Define keyframes at 0%, 33%, 66%, 100% with different color values; the browser interpolates between them smoothly.

intermediate.question: What is the best practice for vendor prefixes with @keyframes?
intermediate.answer: Modern browsers do not require prefixes; for legacy support, duplicate rules with -webkit- prefixes for the @keyframes rule.

intermediate.question: How many keyframe selectors should you use for smooth motion?
intermediate.answer: For simple animations, from/to is sufficient; for complex motion, use 3-5 keyframe points; more than 10 rarely improves quality.

intermediate.question: Can you use the !important keyword inside @keyframes?
intermediate.answer: Yes, !important is allowed but ignored in @keyframes rules; it does not affect the animation values.

intermediate.question: How do you reference a @keyframes animation from multiple elements?
intermediate.answer: Use the same animation-name value on multiple selectors; each element animates independently using the same keyframe definition.

advanced.question: How does the cascade apply to @keyframes in terms of specificity?
advanced.answer: @keyframes have no specificity; the animation property cascade determines which animation applies, but keyframe values override the element's inline styles during animation.

advanced.question: Can @keyframes be dynamically injected via JavaScript?
advanced.answer: Yes, you can inject @keyframes using CSSStyleSheet.insertRule() or constructible stylesheets with adoptedStyleSheets.

advanced.question: How do keyframes with pseudo-elements (::before, ::after) work?
advanced.answer: Apply animation to the pseudo-element selector: .element::before { animation: pulse 2s; } with matching @keyframes.

advanced.question: How do you detect when a keyframe animation reaches a specific point?
advanced.answer: Use the animationiteration event for repeating animations or the animationstart/animationend events for start and completion.

advanced.question: How does the Web Animations API relate to CSS @keyframes?
advanced.answer: The WAAPI can replicate @keyframes behavior: element.animate([{opacity: 0}, {opacity: 1}], {duration: 1000}); provides programmatic control.

advanced.question: How do @keyframes interact with the animation-fill-mode property?
advanced.answer: fill-mode determines whether the element retains keyframe styles before the animation starts (backwards), after it ends (forwards), or both.

advanced.question: Can you nest @keyframes inside other CSS at-rules?
advanced.answer: @keyframes cannot be nested inside @media or @supports; they must be top-level rules in stylesheets.

advanced.question: How do you create a staggered animation effect with @keyframes?
advanced.answer: Use animation-delay with increasing values on multiple elements, or use the same keyframes with different start times via negative delays.

advanced.question: How does the browser optimize @keyframes animations for performance?
advanced.answer: Browsers promote animated elements to compositor layers when animating transform or opacity, running the animation on the compositor thread.

advanced.question: How do you handle @keyframes in shadow DOM contexts?
advanced.answer: @keyframes defined inside shadow DOM are scoped to that shadow tree and do not leak to the light DOM or other shadow roots.
